evals.prompt: I want to build a harness that takes a one-sentence product idea and autonomously builds a full-stack web app over several hours. The app should have a real backend, database, and polished frontend. How should I design this?
evals.expected_output: A detailed harness design with agent architecture, feature list format, context management strategy, and testing approach. Should recommend the three-agent pattern or similar multi-agent setup.

evals.prompt: I need a multi-agent system that generates brand identity packages — logos, color palettes, typography systems, and demo pages — and iterates until the quality is high. The output needs to feel like it came from a real design agency, not AI slop. How do I set this up?
evals.expected_output: A harness design centered on the generator-evaluator pattern with concrete grading criteria for design quality and originality. Should address the self-evaluation problem and include evaluator calibration guidance.

evals.prompt: I want to improve how Claude handles code review on my PRs — should I build a harness for this?
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this doesn't need a full multi-agent harness. A single skill or well-crafted prompt is more appropriate. May suggest lightweight improvements without over-engineering.
